source: Unifies the register's own layers: authored corridors (root *.yaml), computed skeletons (analysis/computed_corridor_skeletons.json), and the event-calendar transition edges. No new facts; compatibility_category is taken from the §5.14 matrix.
note: Directed-edge interaction_sets are the Atlas per-direction refinement and may differ from the undirected §5.14 pair; only the compatibility_category is a hard cross-layer relationship and is enforced. From v0.10.0 every class_code is DERIVED, not authored: tools/class_rule.py:class_of applies the Corridor Atlas v0.2.5 destination-first precedence (prohibition > no-regime > origin-drag > in-transition > operative gate type) to data/signal_table.json, and tools/recompute_classes.py re-derives all 132 and diffs them against this artifact. `materialized_from` is a PRODUCTION-provenance partition (9 authored + 106 skeleton + 17 event-calendar transition edges) and is orthogonal to class: 25 edges now carry an as_of_timeline, not 17.

provenance.basis: Every class_code is recomputed from data/signal_table.json by the published rule tools/class_rule.py:class_of. The rule reads exactly two signals -- the destination's `inbound_gate` and the origin's `token_regime` -- both claim_class=tier1_legal, and never reads `token_in_issue` (tier2_operational). The signal-provenance guarantee is enforced by SIG1 and demonstrated empirically by `recompute_classes.py --prove-tier2-inert`, which flips every market signal and asserts no class moves. Each edge's class_basis records which precedence step decided it, on whose signal, resting on which instrument, at what binding_status.
provenance.record_ref: 132/132 edges carry a class_basis.record_ref into a published node record (123 of them at match=same_instrument); backlog VB-11 is closed for this register, because the 152 node records ARE shipped here and the locator can be supplied rather than declined. `match: related_dimension` marks a locator that is the right dimension but a different instrument from the one the class rests on; every such case, and every binding-status divergence between the signal and the record it cites, is emitted as a finding in analysis/signal_table.json:record_binding_findings rather than reconciled away.

infrastructure_overlap.note: Both ends on Project Agora (US and EU).

inbound_mechanism.test: MiCA EMT authorization wall + Article 58(3) means-of-exchange cap
inbound_mechanism.detail: A non-EUR token faces enhanced reporting and, once significant and used as a means of exchange, the Article 58(3) ceiling (one million transactions/day and EUR 200m/day — both thresholds, per MiCA Art. 23(1) applied mutatis mutandis; EBA operative methodology pending). A EUR sister EMT via a MiCA-authorized issuer avoids the cap.

edges.note: Demand is high and the corridor is operable, but the binding question is scale, not permission. A dollar token can be distributed via a MiCA-authorized issuer, yet once significant and used as a means of exchange it meets the Article 58(3) ceiling; the uncapped route is a euro sister token (Circle's euro authorization is the working example). An unauthorized dollar token cannot be lawfully distributed at all (the Tether delisting lesson).

source.primary: Fan, Cross-Border Digital-Finance Corridor Atlas v0.2.5 (June 2026), §5.1 (United States -> European Union)
source.secondary: Fan, Cross-Border Stablecoin Architecture v3 (Twelve Jurisdictions, June 2026), §5.14 (EU x US: Category I/II hybrid — undirected §5.14 pair; see this edge's `divergence` field)

inbound_mechanism.test: HKMA fiat-referenced-stablecoin licence
inbound_mechanism.detail: Licence required to issue in Hong Kong or to issue an HKD-referenced token anywhere; the first cohort is HKD-only and bank-led, so a non-HKD or crypto-native issuer needs a later licence or a partnership with a licensed issuer.

edges.note: A dollar issuer seeking Hong Kong distribution confronts a first cohort that is HKD-only and bank-led. There is no general inbound recognition of a foreign dollar token for payment use; the operator needs a later HKMA licence for a non-HKD product (timing not public) or a partnership with a licensed HK issuer. The regulatory corridor is the gating archetype; the settlement corridor is contingent on it.

infrastructure_overlap.note: Both ends on Project Agora (US and JP).

inbound_mechanism.test: Japan Electronic Payment Instruments inbound channel-determination
inbound_mechanism.detail: Foreign token circulates only via a registered EPIESP holding reserves in Japan equal to customer holdings, with the foreign issuer not issuing/redeeming/soliciting to Japanese users (recognition + registration, not mutual recognition).

edges.note: A dollar issuer reaching into Japan does not meet automatic dual authorization; it meets a channel. A foreign-issued stablecoin circulates only via a registered EPIESP holding reserves in Japan equal to customer holdings, while the foreign issuer does not issue/redeem/solicit to Japanese users. The working example is USDC, admitted via SBI VC Trade (March 2025). Because the gating step is a recognition + registration channel-determination rather than mutual recognition, the corridor is Category II — the same class as entry into the US or the UAE — even though Japan is a fully live regime.

inbound_mechanism.test: GENIUS Act §18 comparability determination + OCC registration; US-held reserves — enacted 2025-07-18, gate not yet operative (§20)
inbound_mechanism.detail: The §18 pathway (unilateral Treasury comparability determination on SCRC recommendation + OCC registration + US-held reserves) is NOT YET AVAILABLE: per §20 the Act takes effect on the earlier of 2027-01-18 (18 months after enactment) or 120 days after the primary federal payment stablecoin regulators issue final regulations, and at the 2026-06-30 snapshot only proposed rules had issued (OCC NPRM, FR 2026-03-02, comments closed 2026-05-01; FDIC and FinCEN/OFAC NPRMs 2026-04-10; Treasury §4(c) NPRM April 2026). Earlier prose read ‘none granted to date’, which states a MARKET observation (no determination has been made) where a proposition of law is required (the gate is not in force) — the claim-class error Citable by Construction §2.1 exists to prevent.
inbound_mechanism.administrator: US Treasury (Stablecoin Certification Review Committee) / OCC

edges.note: The asymmetric mirror of the dollar-into-Europe corridor, gated differently. A MiCA-authorized euro issuer does not thereby gain US access; entry turns on a §18 Treasury comparability determination, OCC registration, and US-held reserves. The §18 gate is not yet in force (§20: effective on the earlier of 2027-01-18 or final rules + 120 days), so the edge is regime-in-transition today and resolves to Category II on commencement. ‘No determination has been granted’ is a market observation; ‘the gate is not yet available’ is the proposition of law. The asymmetry is structural — the comparability gate is unilateral with no MiCA counterpart.

inbound_mechanism.test: Destination prohibition
inbound_mechanism.detail: No foreign stablecoin may circulate as a payment instrument in the mainland and no mainland entity may issue one. The prohibition is written, in-force law: 银发〔2026〕42号 (eight-ministry Notice, in force 6 Feb 2026; it repealed the 2021 Notice 银发〔2021〕237号) bars unauthorised overseas issuance of RMB-pegged stablecoins (未经许可，境内外任何主体不得在境外发行挂钩人民币的稳定币) and PRC-controlled entities issuing virtual currencies abroad. PRC cross-border data and capital rules (PIPL, DSL, CAC Cross-Border Provisions, SAFE) govern any data or capital leg.

edges.note: The mirror corridor is blocked at the destination. No foreign stablecoin, including an HKD-referenced one, may circulate in the mainland as a payment instrument. Only the regulatory and digital-financial archetypes are meaningful, and only at the level of data and capital flows. The contrast with the (also Category III) PRC->HK direction is the clearest single illustration of why corridors must be directed.

edges.name: HK → Brazil — USD-referenced stablecoin B2B settlement
edges.feasibility_class: Category II (directed edge: channelled — counterparty-conditional + size-capped; the undirected HK-BR pair is I/II)
edges.flow: A USD-referenced stablecoin used as the settlement medium for a B2B cross-border flow, originating on the Hong Kong side and terminating on the Brazil side. Ticket size is the hinge: both Brazil crypto rails are capped per operation, so corridor feasibility differs sharply between sub-cap and above-cap tickets.

sub_gates.label: Issuer side — Stablecoins Ordinance (Cap. 656)
sub_gates.requirement: A licence is required only if the stablecoin is (i) issued in Hong Kong, or (ii) referencing the HKD (in or outside HK), or (iii) actively marketed to the HK public. A USD-referenced coin is NOT HKD-referencing, so limb (ii) does not apply. Limb (i) "issued in HK" is currency-agnostic and turns on the HKMA factor test: place of incorporation; day-to-day management; minting/burning location; where reserve assets are managed; where the mint/redeem bank account is held.
sub_gates.analysis: The regime permits a USD-referenced FRS licence in principle (limb (i) is currency-agnostic), BUT no USD FRS has been licensed: the first cohort (10 Apr 2026) licensed only HKD-referenced issuers — Anchorpoint (FRS01, HKDAP = "HKD At Par") and HSBC (FRS02) — and HKMA commentary signals no near-term USD-pegged path. So for this corridor the live HK route for a USD coin is NOT a domestic FRS licence; it is an offshore-issued non-HKD FRS (e.g. a US issuer) that is not "issued in HK" on the factor test and not actively marketed to HK retail. (Note: HKDAP tracks USD only indirectly — the HKD is pegged to USD at 7.75–7.85 under the Linked Exchange Rate System — but it is legally an HKD instrument and is a different instrument from a USD coin.)

sub_gates.label: Distribution side — permitted offerors
sub_gates.requirement: Offering an FRS does not itself need a stablecoin licence, but only "permitted offerors" may offer an FRS in HK — even one issued offshore / non-HKD: the licensed FRS issuer, authorised institutions (banks), SFC-licensed VATPs, SFC Type 1 corporations, and SVF licensees. An FRS from an issuer not HK-licensed (e.g. a foreign non-HKD FRS) may be offered to professional investors only.
sub_gates.analysis: With no domestic USD FRS available, channel is the operative HK constraint: distribution of the USD coin must run through a permitted offeror, and (because the issuer is not HK-licensed) to professional investors only. The PI-only limb is not binding for B2B — institutional counterparties typically qualify as PIs.

sub_gates.label: Settlement into tokenised funds — SFC securities perimeter
sub_gates.requirement: Where the flow routes a stablecoin balance into a tokenised money-market fund, that fund is a "tokenised security" under the SFO (an interest in a CIS). The SFC's Tokenised Securities Circular (2 Nov 2023, 23EC52/23EC53) applies "same business, same risks, same rules" on a see-through basis; dealing/ distribution needs the relevant SFC licence (Type 1, etc.). Absent SFC authorisation under Part IV SFO / prospectus compliance, the offer is PI-only.
sub_gates.analysis: HK analogue of the US Reves/§404 securities-classification spine: in HK a tokenised MMF is squarely a tokenised security, with SFC-authorised retail instances already live (retail tokenised MMFs authorised Q1 2025). HK gives a clean "yes, it is a security, and here is the regulated path".
sub_gates.gap_note: No HK securities_classification record exists yet — this sub-gate is the spec for it.

boundary_analysis.clears: A B2B USD-stablecoin settlement clears the HK side where: the coin is an offshore non-HKD FRS not actively marketed to HK retail (a domestically-licensed USD FRS does not yet exist — first cohort is HKD-only); it is offered only via a permitted offeror to professional investors (B2B counterparties qualify); and any tokenised- fund leg uses an SFC-authorised tokenised product or is confined to PIs under the tokenised-securities-circular controls.
boundary_analysis.breaks: Issuing a USD-referenced stablecoin in HK (per the factor test) without an HKMA FRS licence; actively marketing the coin to the HK retail public; offering through a non-permitted offeror; or routing into a tokenised MMF/security and distributing it to HK retail without SFC authorisation / prospectus compliance (PI-only otherwise).
sources: Stablecoins Ordinance (Cap. 656), s.14(1) licensing; Schedule 2 minimum criteria

sub_gates.label: eFX rail — closing to crypto on the external leg
sub_gates.requirement: Res 561 (pub. 30 Apr 2026; in force 1 Oct 2026; amends Res 277/2022, which regulates the FX Law, Lei 14.286/2021) bars eFX providers from settling the external leg — provider ↔ foreign counterparty (contraparte no exterior) — with virtual assets; settlement must be via an FX operation or a non-resident BRL (reais) account. eFX is the mass/low-value retail rail: per-operation ceilings of USD 10k (unilateral transfers; same-owner transfers; and, new under 561, financial/capital-market investment transfers); it is not a high-value B2B rail.
sub_gates.analysis: The ban is specific to the eFX settlement leg, not a general stablecoin ban — crypto trading, custody, and other arrangements remain permitted under the VASP regime. The USD 10k eFX ceiling independently confirms eFX was never the natural B2B rail; the practical effect is to foreclose the "reais → USDT → settle abroad on-chain" eFX back-end (the Nomad/Braza-Bank-style rail).

sub_gates.label: VASP-in-FX rail — open but capped
sub_gates.requirement: Res 521 (pub. 10 Nov 2025; in force 2 Feb 2026; amends the FX-law trio Res 277/278/279 that regulate Lei 14.286/2021 — inserting Art. 76-A into Res 277 as the "ativos virtuais no mercado de câmbio" hook) brings authorised VASPs' (SPSAVs') virtual-asset international payments/transfers into the FX market as legitimate câmbio operations, subject to a per-operation cap that bites WHEN THE FOREIGN COUNTERPARTY IS NOT a BCB-FX-authorised institution: USD 100k for a SPSAV; USD 500k for an FX-authorised institution (bank / DTVM / CTVM / corretora de câmbio). Audit + monthly FX reporting to the BCB from 4 May 2026.
sub_gates.analysis: For a USD-stablecoin B2B settlement the foreign counterparty is typically not BCB-FX-authorised, so the cap bites. The cap — not the rail choice — is usually the binding constraint for B2B ticket sizes; above it the flow reverts to traditional câmbio (bank FX desk / DTVM).

sub_gates.label: Custody, segregation & asset curation — Res 520
sub_gates.requirement: Res 520 (in force 2 Feb 2026) requires a documented patrimonial-segregation policy (Art. 28–30): client fiat in individualised accounts; client virtual assets in wallets distinct from the provider's own-operation wallets; the methods used for proof of reserves; and an independent audit of the provider's and clients' virtual assets on a BIENNIAL basis at reasonable-assurance level (Art. 30 III), with the report publicly disclosed on the provider's website (Art. 30 §1). Up to 5% own-asset co-mingling is allowed for immediate liquidity if identified/recorded. Asset curation (Art. 64+) PROHIBITS offering/intermediating algorithmic stablecoins or any fiat-pegged token without effective, verifiable liquid-asset backing; the "ativo de reserva" is defined as fiat + government bonds issued by the same government. Foreign custodians may be used only under home- country authorisation/supervision, with a Brazil representative, enforceable guarantees, formal segregation of Brazilian clients' assets, and record access.
sub_gates.analysis: A USD stablecoin used in this corridor must be an auditable-reserve coin; an algorithmic or opaque-reserve coin is excluded at the Brazilian custody/listing gate regardless of the rail. The foreign-custodian conditions are directly relevant to the HK/offshore custody leg of this corridor.

boundary_analysis.clears: Authorised-VASP-in-FX channel (Res 521), within the per-operation cap (USD 100k SPSAV / USD 500k FX-authorised institution where the foreign counterparty is not BCB-FX-authorised), with audit + monthly FX reporting from 4 May 2026 and Res 520 custody/segregation (Art. 28–30: proof-of-reserves, biennial reasonable-assurance audit, public report), using an auditable-reserve (non-algorithmic) stablecoin.
boundary_analysis.breaks: eFX back-end stablecoin settlement rail (Res 561) — closing 1 Oct 2026. Tickets above the SPSAV / FI per-operation caps (no crypto rail available — reverts to traditional câmbio). Algorithmic or non-auditable-reserve stablecoins (excluded by Res 520 asset-curation rules, Art. 64+).

edges.key_constraint: The binding constraint is regulatory geography COMPOUNDED BY A SIZING CEILING, and the two legs gate on different axes. Brazil offers no single uniform crypto rail: the eFX mass-payment rail's external settlement leg is closing to crypto (Res 561, 1 Oct 2026) and is in any case capped at USD 10k/op, so a stablecoin B2B flow must use the authorised- VASP-in-FX channel (Res 521) — itself capped per operation (USD 100k SPSAV / USD 500k FX-authorised institution, where the foreign counterparty is not BCB-FX-authorised) and limited to auditable-reserve, non-algorithmic stablecoins (Res 520). Above those caps, neither crypto rail is available and the flow reverts to traditional câmbio. Hong Kong gates the SAME instrument on a different axis — not a numeric cap but a perimeter/ eligibility line: issued-in-HK FRS licensing, permitted-offeror distribution, professional-investor-only for non-HKD FRS (and, today, the absence of any licensed USD FRS — first cohort is HKD-only), and (if the flow touches a tokenised fund) the SFC securities perimeter. A single USD-stablecoin instrument therefore cannot serve both legs as one uniform rail: HK gates on WHO may issue/offer and TO WHOM; Brazil gates on WHICH rail and UP TO WHAT SIZE. Four-way composition (rail × cap × auditable-reserve × HK eligibility), not a single prohibition.
edges.us_doctrinal_link: Both legs turn on the same question as US §404's bona-fide-activity carve-out: what activity is permitted on the regulated stablecoin rail. Brazil answers by rail-and-cap (eFX closed; VASP-in-FX open but capped), with a parallel securities axis: the BCB/CVM jurisdictional split (Decreto 11.563/2023 preserves CVM authority over securities-like tokens) is the Brazilian analogue of the US Reves/§404 securities-classification spine — though Brazil's C4 contribution is currently token-classification + asset-curation (Res 520), not yet a developed routing-into-tokenised-fund story (the CVM-175 fund analogue would be the true parallel to the US 1940-Act MMF routing question). HK answers by perimeter-and-eligibility and treats a tokenised MMF as a tokenised security. US remains the anchor instance; HK and Brazil are two contrasting resolutions of the identical boundary.

edges.phase_in_note: Brazil is in rolling implementation: Res 561 in force 1 Oct 2026; non-authorised-provider prohibition and eFX-modality Unicad registration 30 Oct 2026; unauthorised eFX providers must seek payment-institution authorisation by 31 May 2027; Travel Rule fully phased by 2 Feb 2028; IOF treatment unresolved. A reader after mid-2026 should re-verify these moving parts before relying on the corridor.
edges.reviewed_against: Firm/practitioner analysis of BCB Res 519/520/521 + 561 (Notabene, Cescon, BDO, Global Legal Insights, Conduit) and the official BCB normativo URLs (confirmed to exist); the article-level pinpoints (e.g. Res 520 Art 29/30, Res 561 ementa amending Res 277/2022) are taken from those firm summaries and NOT yet read against the official DOU/BCB text. Statutory base: Lei 14.286/2021 (FX) + Lei 14.478/2022 + Decreto 11.563/2023; the câmbio-inclusion hook Art. 76-A is a provision inserted into Res 277/2022 by Res 521 (confirmed against the Res 521 DOU text, Jun 2026 — not, as some firm summaries imply, an article of Lei 14.286/2021). HK side: Cap.656 + HKMA Explanatory Note + SFC 23EC52/23EC53; the HKDAP=HKD-referenced fact is confirmed from the Standard Chartered/Anchorpoint press release (Tier 1), HSBC HKD-referenced.

inbound_mechanism.detail: The §18 pathway (unilateral Treasury comparability determination on SCRC recommendation + OCC registration + US-held reserves) is NOT YET AVAILABLE: per §20 the Act takes effect on the earlier of 2027-01-18 (18 months after enactment) or 120 days after the primary federal payment stablecoin regulators issue final regulations, and at the 2026-06-30 snapshot only proposed rules had issued (OCC NPRM, FR 2026-03-02, comments closed 2026-05-01; FDIC and FinCEN/OFAC NPRMs 2026-04-10; Treasury §4(c) NPRM April 2026). Earlier prose read ‘none granted to date’, which states a MARKET observation (no determination has been made) where a proposition of law is required (the gate is not in force) — the claim-class error Citable by Construction §2.1 exists to prevent.

inbound_mechanism.test: GENIUS Act §18 comparability determination + OCC registration; US-held reserves — enacted 2025-07-18, gate not yet operative (§20)
inbound_mechanism.detail: The §18 pathway (unilateral Treasury comparability determination on SCRC recommendation + OCC registration + US-held reserves) is NOT YET AVAILABLE: per §20 the Act takes effect on the earlier of 2027-01-18 (18 months after enactment) or 120 days after the primary federal payment stablecoin regulators issue final regulations, and at the 2026-06-30 snapshot only proposed rules had issued (OCC NPRM, FR 2026-03-02, comments closed 2026-05-01; FDIC and FinCEN/OFAC NPRMs 2026-04-10; Treasury §4(c) NPRM April 2026). Earlier prose read ‘none granted to date’, which states a MARKET observation (no determination has been made) where a proposition of law is required (the gate is not in force) — the claim-class error Citable by Construction §2.1 exists to prevent.

edges.note: The asymmetric mirror. A yen Electronic Payment Instrument (issued by a bank, registered funds-transfer provider, or trust bank) is freely exportable, but entering the US it meets the §18 comparability gate. The §18 comparability gate is enacted but not yet operative (§20), so the corridor is regime-in-transition today and resolves to Category II on commencement — for a different reason than inbound: there the channel is the gate, here the comparability determination is.
